trips.name: Flemish Art Cities
trips.description: The Flemish triangle of Bruges, Ghent, and Antwerp contains more world-class medieval art per square kilometre than almost anywhere on earth. Drive between canal-laced cities to see Van Eyck's Ghent Altarpiece, Bruegel's Flemish landscapes in Brussels, Rubens' cathedral masterpieces in Antwerp, and Bruges' mirror-still waterways lined with medieval guild houses.
trips.cover_search_query: Bruges Belgium canals medieval guild houses reflection

trips.name: Belgian Ardennes
trips.description: The Belgian Ardennes is a dense hardwood plateau of river valleys, limestone caves, and ruined castles that stretches into Luxembourg — Belgium's most dramatic scenery. The Ourthe and Semois rivers carve deep meanders below forested ridgelines; Bouillon's castle rises on a rock above the river, and the caves of Han-sur-Lesse contain one of Europe's finest show cave systems.
trips.cover_search_query: Belgian Ardennes Bouillon castle river valley forest

trips.name: Dutch Tulip Fields & Keukenhof
trips.description: Each spring the Dutch bulb fields burst into candy-striped carpets of colour visible from the air — tulips, hyacinths, and daffodils stretching to the horizon between Haarlem and Leiden. Keukenhof is the world's largest flower garden; the nearby dune coast road runs through nature reserves where you can cycle between windmills, cheese markets, and fish-smoking villages.
trips.cover_search_query: Dutch tulip fields Netherlands colourful stripes windmill spring

trips.name: Luxembourg's Mullerthal — Little Switzerland
trips.description: The Mullerthal region in eastern Luxembourg earns its nickname 'Little Switzerland' through a labyrinth of mossy sandstone cliffs, narrow gorges, and waterfalls hiding in beech forests. The long-distance Mullerthal Trail threads through this landscape; the fairy-tale village of Vianden and Luxembourg City's UNESCO cliff-top old town bookend a route of striking contrasts.
highlights: Vianden castle on the Our river
trips.cover_search_query: Mullerthal Luxembourg sandstone gorge moss forest Little Switzerland

trips.name: Zeeland Delta Islands
trips.description: Zeeland — Sea Land — is the Netherlands' island province, its peninsulas and former islands now connected by the Delta Works, the greatest feat of hydraulic engineering in human history. Drive the Deltaweg across vast storm surge barriers above open water, explore Middelburg's restored medieval centre, and watch kitesurfers and oystercatchers share the tidal mudflats of the Oosterschelde.
trips.cover_search_query: Zeeland Delta Works Netherlands storm surge barrier sea

trips.name: Wallonia Castles & Meuse Valley
trips.description: The Meuse river bisects Wallonia through a valley of dramatic limestone cliffs and riverside fortresses built by prince-bishops of Liège and dukes of Namur over centuries of border warfare. Château de Freyr rises above terraced gardens on the river bank; the citadel of Namur commands the confluence of Meuse and Sambre; Liège is a passionate industrial city of Walloon pride and magnificent churches.
trips.cover_search_query: Meuse Valley Belgium castle Namur citadel Wallonia river

trips.name: Luxembourg Moselle Wine Road
trips.description: Luxembourg's 40km Moselle valley may be small but it punches far above its weight — a gentle corridor of south-facing vineyards producing outstanding Riesling, Auxerrois, and sparkling Crémant that rivals Champagne at a fraction of the price. Drive the wine route through Roman villages, stop at family estates for tastings, and take an afternoon river boat from Remich.
trips.cover_search_query: Luxembourg Moselle valley vineyards wine Remich river

trips.name: Hoge Veluwe & Gelderland
trips.description: The Hoge Veluwe is the Netherlands' largest national park — a surprising inland wilderness of heather moorland, sand drifts, and pine forest, home to wild boar, red deer, and the extraordinary Kröller-Müller Museum with the world's second-largest Van Gogh collection. Arnhem and Nijmegen are nearby — cities shaped by Operation Market Garden whose wartime heritage still marks the landscape.
trips.cover_search_query: Hoge Veluwe Netherlands heather moorland national park deer

trips.name: Maastricht & the Limburg Hills
trips.description: Dutch Limburg is an anomaly — a narrow tongue of land jutting south between Belgium and Germany where the landscape actually undulates. Maastricht is the Netherlands' most Continental city, its Burgundian café culture, Roman catacombs, and medieval fortifications reflecting centuries at a European crossroads. The Valkenburg caves and Geuldal valley complete a route unlike anything else in the Netherlands.
trips.cover_search_query: Maastricht Netherlands medieval city Meuse river Vrijthof square

trips.name: North Holland Dunes & Texel Island
trips.description: North of Amsterdam the coast narrows between the IJsselmeer and the North Sea into a corridor of dune reserves, polders, and traditional fishing villages. Alkmaar's cheese market is a living piece of Dutch folklore; the ferry to Texel leads to the Wadden Sea's largest island, with wide beaches, seals, migrating birds, and organic sheep farms.
highlights: Texel seal beaches & Wadden UNESCO site
trips.cover_search_query: Texel island Netherlands Wadden Sea beach lighthouse dunes

trips.name: Vianden & the Our Valley
trips.description: The Our river forms Luxembourg's border with Germany through a wild valley of forested ridges and steep meadows that feels far from any capital. Vianden's castle — one of the largest feudal residences in Europe — rises on a rock above the river town where Victor Hugo spent a summer in exile. The Our valley hiking trails lead into the Eifel volcanic plateau across the border.
highlights: Clervaux Family of Man photography exhibition
trips.cover_search_query: Vianden castle Luxembourg Our river valley medieval

trips.name: Belgian Coast — Dunes & Belle Époque
trips.description: Belgium's 67km coast is compact but rich — a string of resorts from the wild dunes of De Panne near France to the sophisticated canal town of Knokke near the Dutch border. The coastal tram (world's longest regular tram route) connects them all; the Westhoek dunes are a rare ecosystem; and Ostend's art nouveau heritage and North Sea fish restaurants make it Belgium's most characterful seaside town.
trips.cover_search_query: Belgian coast Ostend North Sea beach tram dunes

trips.name: Dutch Waterways & Amsterdam Surroundings
trips.description: The Netherlands is a country built on water — a precise, centuries-old engineering project of dykes, windmills, and pumping stations that holds back the sea from land below sea level. This loop from Amsterdam takes in Kinderdijk's iconic 19 windmills, the cheese markets of Gouda, the pottery workshops of Delft, and the royal palace gardens of The Hague.
trips.cover_search_query: Kinderdijk windmills Netherlands UNESCO reflection canal

trips.name: East Belgium — Eifel Highlands & Spa
trips.description: Eastern Belgium's German-speaking cantons are a quiet surprise — a high plateau of peat bogs, signal lakes, and ancient market towns absorbed from Germany after World War I. The Hautes Fagnes moorland is Belgium's highest and wildest terrain; Spa gave its name to the entire concept of health resorts and still draws visitors to its mineral springs; and Liège rewards with a passionate street food scene.
highlights: Hautes Fagnes peat bogs & Signal de Botrange
trips.cover_search_query: Hautes Fagnes Belgium high moor peat bog Signal de Botrange

trips.name: Groningen & the Frisian Islands
trips.description: The far north of the Netherlands feels like another country — flat, windswept, and proud. Groningen's vibrant student city and world-class Groninger Museum sit in a province of terp mounds and medieval churches. Ferry out to Vlieland and Terschelling in the Wadden Sea, where car-free islands of sand and forest harbour some of Europe's finest undisturbed birdwatching.
trips.cover_search_query: Terschelling Netherlands Wadden Sea island beach dunes ferry

trips.name: Belgian Hageland & Haspengouw Orchards
trips.description: The Hageland and Haspengouw are Belgium's hidden countryside — rolling hills of orchards and vineyards east of Brussels that explode into white blossom in early spring. This fertile region produces Belgium's emerging wines, its famous Lambic beer hops, and the country's finest stone fruit. Leuven's world-class Gothic university town anchors a route of beer abbeys, blossom routes, and medieval castles.
trips.cover_search_query: Haspengouw Belgium apple blossom orchards spring Limburg

trips.name: Dutch Rivers — Rhine, Waal & Maas
trips.description: The great rivers of Europe — Rhine, Waal, and Meuse — fan out across the Netherlands in a braided delta of islands, ferry crossings, and river dyke roads. The Betuwe ('good land') between the rivers is an orchard landscape of cherry and pear blossom; Nijmegen is the oldest city in the Netherlands; and the fort island of Loevestein guards the confluence where Waal meets the Maas.
trips.cover_search_query: Betuwe Netherlands fruit blossom orchards river dyke polder

trips.name: Brussels & Brabant Heritage
trips.description: Beyond Brussels' Grand Place and European quarter, Brabant hides a ring of forest towns, baroque abbeys, and Waterloo's historic battlefield. Drive south to Waterloo where Napoleon's empire ended in the mud; visit the extraordinary pilgrimage chapel of Grimbergen; and loop back through the Sonian Forest — one of Europe's oldest beech forests and a UNESCO World Heritage site — to Brussels' art nouveau heart.
trips.cover_search_query: Villers-la-Ville Belgium Cistercian abbey ruins romantic forest

trips.name: Luxembourg Ardennes — Esch-sur-Sûre & South
trips.description: Southern Luxembourg is less visited than the Mullerthal but equally rewarding — the Sûre river loops around the dramatic rocky spur of Esch-sur-Sûre with its medieval castle ruin, and the Upper Sûre Natural Park contains one of the Grand Duchy's most beautiful lakes. The market town of Wiltz, famous for its open-air theatre festival, makes an ideal base.
highlights: Bastogne Battle of the Bulge museum
trips.cover_search_query: Esch-sur-Sûre Luxembourg castle river loop meander Ardennes

trips.name: Friesland — Lakes, Windmills & Elfstedentocht
trips.description: Friesland has its own language, its own culture, and its own epic winter skating race — the Elfstedentocht — linking eleven historic cities across 200km of frozen canals. In summer the same waterways are the playground of the Netherlands' most beloved sailing region, while the terraced-field landscape of the coastal clay marshes hides terp-mound villages with outstanding Romanesque churches.
trips.cover_search_query: Friesland Netherlands sailing lakes windmill canal Dutch
